mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes CLA45 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned CLA45 AMG (M133) engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A rebuilt engine typically costs £5,500–£9,000. Main dealer new engines start from £9,000–£15,000+ supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes CLA45 AMG engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M133 has a mixed reputation. It's a hand-built performance engine that was once the world's most powerful production four-cylinder. However, it has well-documented weaknesses: turbocharger failure is the most common and costly issue, timing chain stretch is a documented concern, and carbon buildup and oil leaks are also common. With proper maintenance, these engines can be reliable, but the turbocharger issues are a genuine concern.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes CLA45 AMG?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Turbocharger failure — wastegate bushing wear, mounting bracket fractures, and oil feed line contamination, (2) Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure — causing cold-start rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (3) Carbon buildup on intake valves — causing misfires and flat spots, and (4) Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and oil filter housing.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes CLA45 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £12,000–£22,000 CLA45 AMG, a £5,000–£7,500 recon is 25–60% of the car's value. For a clean example with full service history, replacement is often worth it given the CLA45's strong retained values and enthusiast following. However, if the car has other issues (transmission, electrical faults, accident damage), evaluate total repair costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes CLA45 AMG engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The CLA45 AMG uses the M133 engine code — a 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder. Early models (2013–2015) produce 360 PS (265 kW) and 450 Nm; facelift models (2015–2019) produce 381 PS (280 kW) and 475 Nm. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent turbocharger failure on the M133?

acceptedAnswer.text: Prevention is not guaranteed, but you can reduce the risk: (1) Change oil every 10,000 miles or less with the correct MB-spec oil (0W-40 or 5W-40) — turbochargers are oil-dependent, (2) Allow the engine to idle for 30–60 seconds before switching off to let the turbo cool, (3) Use high-quality premium fuel (RON 98 or higher), and (4) Never ignore whistling noises or power loss — book an inspection immediately. Some owners recommend the installation of an anti-surge valve to reduce compressor surge and turbo damage.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes CLA45 AMG Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLA45 AMG engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The transverse engine layout and AMG-specific components add complexity.

cta.label: → Compare CLA45 AMG engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Turbocharger Failure (Wastegate & Mounting Bracket)
problems.symptoms: Power loss, whistling or siren-like noise from the engine bay, oil in the intake, rattling noise, restricted performance
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — can occur earlier on tuned examples
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (turbocharger rebuild or replacement; wastegate bushing repair)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you hear whistling or experience power loss

problems.recommendation: Turbocharger issues are one of the most common problems on the M133 engine. A worn turbocharger wastegate flapper bushing can cause rattling. A fractured turbo mounting bracket is also documented. Mercedes initiated a Service Campaign for CLA45 AMG turbocharger failure due to contamination of the oil feed line. One owner reported that a turbocharger failure at 45,000 miles required an engine rebuild. At the first sign of power loss or unusual noises, have the turbo system inspected immediately. A £1,500–£3,500 repair is far cheaper than a £5,500–£11,000 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full turbo failure guide → /failures/m133-turbo-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch & Tensioner Failure
problems.symptoms: Rattling or clanking noise on cold start for a few seconds, erratic idle, engine misfires, check engine light, reduced power
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — can occur with insufficient oil pressure or infrequent oil changes
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 (timing chain tensioner replacement) / £2,000–£4,000 (full timing chain kit)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Timing chain stretch is a documented issue on the M133 engine. A failing timing chain tensioner can cause rattling or clanking, especially on startup. If the tensioner fails completely, the timing chain can jump teeth, allowing valves and pistons to collide. At the first sign of cold-start rattling, get the tensioner and chain inspected immediately. A £800–£2,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£11,000 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain tensioner guide

problems.title: Carbon Build-Up (Direct Injection Engines)
problems.symptoms: Misfires, hesitation or flat spots during acceleration, rough idle, reduced power, poor fuel economy
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: Like all direct-injection engines, the M133 is prone to carbon buildup on intake valves. This can cause misfires and flat spots during acceleration. Walnut blasting is the recommended cleaning method. If you experience rough running or hesitation, have the intake valves inspected. Regular use of premium fuel and occasional Italian tune-ups can help mitigate carbon buildup.

failureLink.label: Read the full carbon buildup guide → /failures/carbon-buildup-intake/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Oil Leaks & Fuel System Issues
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell, visible oil residue around the valve cover or oil filter housing, low oil level warning; fuel pump issues causing low power
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (oil leaks — valve cover gasket or oil filter housing) / £800–£2,000 (fuel pump replacement)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are a common issue on the M133, often stemming from the valve cover gasket or oil filter housing. One owner reported an oil leak from the oil cooler and filter assembly at 65,000 miles. Fuel system issues are also documented — the M133 has no separate fuel filter, and the intake strainer can become blocked, causing low fuel pressure and reduced performance. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Catch leaks early to prevent more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide → /failures/m133-oil-leaks/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: CLA45 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch early to prevent engine damage
Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means full replacement required
Catch early to prevent engine damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLA45 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using correct MB-spec oil (0W-40 or 5W-40)
* No turbocharger noise or power loss — turbo issues are the most common M133 problem
* No cold-start rattling — this is a critical warning sign of timing chain wear
* Remainder of manufacturer warranty or extended warranty in place
avoidIf: * Whistling or siren-like noise from the engine — turbocharger failure risk
* Cold-start rattle with no documented timing chain work
* Poor service history — the M133 is a high-performance engine that needs proper maintenance
* Check engine light with misfire or timing-related codes

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes CLA45 AMG is a genuine performance icon — a hand-built 2.0-litre turbo four-cylinder that was once the world's most powerful production four-cylinder. The M133 engine delivers up to 381 hp and 475 Nm, propelling the CLA45 to 60 mph in just 4.2 seconds. However, it has well-documented weaknesses: turbocharger failure is the most common and costly issue, timing chain stretch is a documented concern, and carbon buildup and oil leaks are also common. Buy a well-maintained example with full service history and no rattling. Without history? Proceed with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare CLA45 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes CLA45 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or clanking from the engine (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Turbo Health — Listen for whistling or siren-like noises under acceleration; check for power loss
* Service History — Including the M133 engine and confirmed oil change intervals (0W-40 or 5W-40 MB-spec)
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve cover and oil filter housing
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for timing, turbo or misfire-related codes
* Carbon Buildup — Any hesitation or flat spots under acceleration? (Intake valve carbon)
* Fuel System — Any low power or jerky running? (Fuel pump or strainer issues)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
